title: Delicious Crisp Filipino Pork Dinuguan
description: A winey, crisp, and flavorful Filipino dish made with pork, coconut milk, and beurre-monte, perfect for meal prep and low-sodium diets.
instructions: In a large pot, combine pork blood, pork belly, coconut milk, beurre-monte, vinegar, salt, black pepper, garlic, onion, and bay leaves.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, stirring occasionally.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 30 minutes, or until the pork is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the bay leaves and stir in additional vinegar if desired.
Increase the heat to high and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the sauce has reached the desired consistency.
Serve the dinuguan hot, garnished with chopped green onions and steamed rice on the side.
Let the dish cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for meal prep.
Reheat the dinuguan over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
